name: Japanese Fruitcake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour four 9-inch round cake pans.
2. Cream butter until smooth. Gradually add sugar, beating well at medium speed with an electric mixer.
3. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
4. Combine flour and baking powder; stir into the creamed mixture alternately with milk, beginning and ending with the milk. Mix after each addition.
5. Stir in vanilla extract.
6. Divide batter evenly between two of the cake pans. In the other two pans, stir spices, raisins, and pecans into the batter.
7. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
8. Allow cakes to cool in the pans for 20 minutes before removing them and cooling completely on wire racks.
9. Prick the cooled layers with a wooden pick at 2-inch intervals.
10. Spread filling/frosting between layers and on top and sides of the cake, stacking layers with a light layer on top of each spiced layer.
11. Garnish with pecan halves if desired.
For Filling/Frosting:
1. In a medium saucepan, combine sugar and flour. Stir in remaining ingredients until smooth.
2. Bring mixture to a boil over medium heat, stirring constantly.
3. Cook for 5 minutes or until thickened, stirring constantly. Allow the filling/frosting to cool completely before using.
Nutritional analysis per serving: 741 calories, 32 grams fat, 112 grams carbohydrates, 115 milligrams cholesterol, 406 milligrams sodium, 38 percent of calories from fat.
